body: Went with 15-min access tokens + 30-day refresh in an httpOnly SameSite=Lax cookie. Login is rate limited to 5/min per IP. Tests green.
Nice. Can we surface a clearer error than 'invalid credentials' when the account just isn't verified yet? Confused me in testing.
Keyset pagination is in - cursor is base64(created_at,id). Way faster than OFFSET on my seed data of 10k bookmarks.
Reading pane feels good. Bumped line-height to 1.7 and capped measure at 68ch for readability. Shortcuts cheatsheet shows on '?'.
Love it. Merged after the keyboard map tweak. This is the feature I'll actually use daily.
Outbox pattern working - search results show up within ~2s of saving a bookmark. Algolia free tier is plenty for now.
Found it - worker wasn't committing the outbox cursor until the whole batch flushed, so a restart replayed creates but dropped deletes. Moved to per-event ack.
Reindexed prod and confirmed deleted links are gone from search. Closing.
Chart deploys cleanly to the k3s box. Cloudflare tunnel ingress is up at ridgelinesystems.com. Reindex CronJob runs nightly at 3am PT.
Checkout flow stubbed against Stripe test mode. Still need to gate Pro features and handle the subscription cancel webhook. Picking back up tonight.
Root cause: the logging middleware buffered the body before the Stripe handler could verify the signature. Reading raw body first with io.ReadAll then re-setting it. PR up for review.
Thinking Klaviyo over Mailchimp here since the segment API is nicer. Will spike both before committing.
Fixed the FOUC by inlining a tiny theme script in index.html that reads localStorage before paint. No more flash.
Closing as wont-do. The bookmark naming is already in the public API docs and the rename buys us nothing. Not worth the migration.
Confirmed the leak with pprof - one goroutine per IP, none ever returning. Replaced with a single sweep goroutine that evicts stale buckets every minute. Memory flat now.
Mocked up the settings layout in Figma - tabbed: Profile / Security / Data. Sharing the link in Discord. Data export as a download button bottom-right.
Layout looks great. I'll wire the export endpoint to stream JSON so big accounts don't blow memory.
Poller supports HTTP and raw TCP checks, interval per target in the config YAML. Storing results in Postgres with a 90-day retention window.
Sparkline component done - using a tiny inline SVG instead of a chart lib to keep the bundle small. Need real data from the poller to tune the buckets.
Want the Discord ping to fire only after 3 consecutive failures so a single blip doesn't wake me at 2am. Will reuse the webhook from our gaming server.
